Issue #5: Extract Reusable ChatShell Logic Component

Visual Architecture Review

Executive Summary

This slice extracts chat orchestration logic from Chat.razor into a new reusable ChatShell.razor component. This enables both full-page chat and mini-chat widgets to share a common state management and streaming response handler.

Goal: Create a headless logic component that owns message state, streaming orchestration, and session management while keeping UI concerns in Chat.razor.

Architecture: Before & After

Current State

Chat.razor
Monolithic Page Component
  • Page route & layout
  • Message display UI
  • Chat orchestration logic
  • Streaming response handling
  • Session state
  • File uploads
  • All concerns mixed

⚠️ Tightly coupled logic makes reuse difficult

Proposed State

Chat.razor Refactored
UI Presentation Layer
  • Page route & layout
  • Message display UI
  • File upload preprocessing
  • Delegates orchestration to ChatShell
ChatShell.razor NEW
Logic & State Layer (Headless)
  • Message state management
  • Streaming orchestration
  • Session ID handling
  • Reset & cancel flows
  • Reusable by mini-chat & full-page

Component Hierarchy & Data Flow

flowchart TB subgraph UILayer["UI Layer (Chat.razor)"] MessageList["📋 Message List Display"] ChatInput["⌨️ Chat Input Field"] FileAttach["📎 File Attachment UI"] end subgraph LogicLayer["Logic Layer (ChatShell.razor)"] StateManagement["🔄 State Management
(messages, session, currentResponse)"] StreamingHandler["🌊 Streaming Handler
(orchestration, cancellation)"] ResetFlow["↻ Reset & Cancel Flows"] end subgraph Services["External Services"] ChatClient["🤖 IChatClient
(GetStreamingResponseAsync)"] FileService["☁️ FileUploadService"] end MessageList -->|Binds to| StateManagement ChatInput -->|Calls| AddUserMessage["AddUserMessageAsync()"] FileAttach -->|Calls| FileService FileService -->|Result to| AddUserMessage AddUserMessage -->|Updates| StateManagement StateManagement -->|Streams via| StreamingHandler StreamingHandler -->|Calls| ChatClient ChatClient -->|Response Stream| StreamingHandler

Component Responsibilities

Chat.razor (UI Layer)

Keeps:

  • Page route & Blazor layout
  • Message list rendering
  • Chat input component
  • File attachment UX

Delegates:

  • Message state → ChatShell
  • Streaming logic → ChatShell
  • Session management → ChatShell
ChatShell.razor (Logic Layer) NEW

Owns:

  • Message list state
  • Current response tracking
  • Streaming orchestration
  • Session & conversation IDs
  • Cancel & reset semantics

Exposes:

  • Public API for Chat.razor
  • Callbacks (OnMessageAdded)
  • State accessors (readonly)

ChatShell Public API Contract

// Parameters
[Parameter] public string? ConversationId { get; set; }
[Parameter] public EventCallback<ChatMessage> OnMessageAdded { get; set; }

// State Accessors (Read-Only)
public IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> Messages => messages;
public ChatMessage? CurrentResponseMessage => currentResponseMessage;
public string CurrentConversationId => sessionId;

// Public Methods
public Task AddUserMessageAsync(
    ChatMessage userMessage, 
    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default);

public Task ResetConversationAsync();
public void CancelAnyCurrentResponse();
Member Purpose Consumed By
Messages All user + assistant messages Chat.razor message list rendering
CurrentResponseMessage In-progress assistant streaming message Chat.razor partial response display
CurrentConversationId Active session ID (effective value) Chat.razor for session tracking
AddUserMessageAsync() Add user message & trigger streaming response Chat.razor on user input submit
ResetConversationAsync() Clear messages & generate new session Chat.razor "New Chat" button
CancelAnyCurrentResponse() Stop in-progress streaming Chat.razor cancel/stop button

User Interaction Flow

Send Message Sequence:

1
User enters text & optional file in Chat.razor input
2
Chat.razor uploads file (if attached) via FileUploadService
3
Chat.razor calls ChatShell.AddUserMessageAsync(userMessage)
4
ChatShell adds user message to state, fires OnMessageAdded callback
5
ChatShell calls IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()
6
ChatShell updates CurrentResponseMessage on each token chunk
7
Chat.razor re-renders real-time as CurrentResponseMessage updates
8
ChatShell finalizes response when stream completes, fires OnMessageAdded

Cancel Flow:

1
User clicks Cancel button in Chat.razor
2
Chat.razor calls ChatShell.CancelAnyCurrentResponse()
3
ChatShell triggers cancellation token, appends partial content (preserves current behavior)
4
Chat.razor re-renders without CurrentResponseMessage

Reset Flow:

1
User clicks New Chat button in Chat.razor
2
Chat.razor calls ChatShell.ResetConversationAsync()
3
ChatShell clears messages, generates new SessionId, adds system prompt
4
Chat.razor re-renders with empty message list

Implementation Task Breakdown

Task 1: Create ChatShell.razor
Extract and scaffold the new component
  • Copy current Chat.razor structure as base
  • Extract message state: messages, currentResponseMessage, sessionId
  • Move orchestration methods: AddUserMessageAsync, streaming loop, cancel/reset
  • Add public accessors: Messages, CurrentResponseMessage, CurrentConversationId
  • Add @parameter directives: ConversationId, OnMessageAdded
  • Keep all existing logging and error handling
Task 2: Refactor Chat.razor
Adapt to use ChatShell via @ref
  • Declare @ref ChatShell chatShell instance
  • Replace state fields with chatShell.Messages and chatShell.CurrentResponseMessage bindings
  • Update ChatInput.OnSend handler to call chatShell.AddUserMessageAsync()
  • Retain file upload preprocessing in Chat.razor
  • Update "New Chat" button to call chatShell.ResetConversationAsync()
  • Update "Cancel" button to call chatShell.CancelAnyCurrentResponse()
Task 3: Build & Validate
Ensure no regressions
  • Run dotnet build InterviewCoach.slnx
  • Fire up local app stack via Aspire
  • Smoke test: send text message → verify stream appears
  • Smoke test: attach file → verify URL in outbound content
  • Smoke test: click New Chat → verify reset & new session
  • Smoke test: send message, click Cancel → verify partial append behavior

Key Design Decisions

1. File Upload Stays in Chat.razor (for Slice 1)

File preprocessing (read file, upload via FileUploadService, append URL to text) remains in Chat.razor to avoid expanding ChatShell's initial scope. ChatShell accepts already-prepared user messages.

2. ConversationId Parameter Precedence

If ConversationId is supplied on init, ChatShell uses it. On reset, a new ID is generated unless external override is requested in a future slice. This prevents ambiguity.

3. OnMessageAdded Callback Semantics

Fires only for complete message additions (user message add, finalized assistant message), NOT on every token update. This keeps callbacks discrete and prevents callback noise.

4. No Mini-Chat Wired in Slice 1

ChatShell API is designed for reuse by mini-chat, but actual mini-chat integration is deferred to Slice 2 (FloatingChatWidget). Slice 1 proves the abstraction on full-page chat only.

Risks & Mitigations

Risk 1: Streaming Race Conditions

Cancellation while stream is updating could duplicate or reorder message fragments.

Mitigation: Keep cancellation and append semantics identical to current Chat.razor implementation. No new logic = no new race conditions.

Risk 2: ConversationId Ownership Ambiguity

Conflict between externally supplied ConversationId and internally generated session IDs.

Mitigation: Document parameter behavior in XML comments. Define clear precedence rule: external parameter wins on init, internal generation on reset.

Risk 3: Component Ref Lifecycle

@ref access timing issues if Chat.razor and ChatShell lifecycle don't align.

Mitigation: Keep ChatShell as a child of Chat.razor (direct component hierarchy). Validate @ref availability in OnAfterRender hook.

Verification Plan

Build Verification

dotnet build InterviewCoach.slnx

Manual Functional Tests (on /interview page)

Test Scenario Expected Behavior Pass?
Send text message Message appears in list, streamed response updates incrementally, matches current behavior —
Attach file & send File uploads, URL appended to outbound content, response streams normally —
Click New Chat Message list clears, new SessionId generated, UI resets, system prompt sent —
Send message, click Cancel mid-stream Current partial response is appended to message, stream stops, UI updates —
Check browser console No errors or unhandled exceptions —
Test JSON parse error handling Graceful fallback message displayed (current behavior preserved) —

Acceptance Criteria (Issue #5)

✓
Create ChatShell with public message/current-response accessors
Messages, CurrentResponseMessage, CurrentConversationId properties
✓
Implement AddUserMessageAsync with streaming response handling
Orchestrates IChatClient calls, updates CurrentResponseMessage on tokens
✓
Implement ResetConversationAsync and CancelAnyCurrentResponse
Clear state, generate new session, cancel in-flight streams
✓
Expose ConversationId and OnMessageAdded parameters
Parameter properties and EventCallback for external wiring
✓
Build succeeds for InterviewCoach.slnx
No compilation errors, integration with existing components

Optional Follow-Up (Recommended for Slice 2)

Hardening Tests
Focused unit & integration tests
  • ChatShell message ordering under concurrent sends
  • Cancellation + streaming token append semantics
  • Session ID generation and reset cycles
  • OnMessageAdded callback firing granularity
  • Error handling for network failures mid-stream

These tests will reduce regression risk before Slice 2 wires mini-chat to ChatShell.
